g: verb — to do work, to handle affairs; to hold a job
gv: động từ — làm việc, xử lý công việc; đi làm, có việc làm

g: verb — to hold an informal discussion seated together; noun — a forum, a panel talk
gv: động từ — ngồi lại cùng nhau trò chuyện, bàn bạc thoải mái; danh từ — buổi tọa đàm, buổi nói chuyện chuyên đề

g: preposition — as; in the capacity of; serving as; verb — to act as; to function as
gv: giới từ — với tư cách là; trong vai trò; động từ — làm; đóng vai

g: noun — a seat, a place to sit
noun — a written composition, essay; verb — to write an essay
gv: danh từ — bài văn, bài tập làm văn; động từ — viết văn

g: noun — crop, cultivated plant
gv: danh từ — cây trồng, hoa màu

g: verb — to sit down
noun — homework, assignment; (also) an operation, task to carry out
gv: danh từ — bài tập về nhà; (cũng) công việc, thao tác cần làm

g: noun — function; effect; role; influence
gv: danh từ — tác dụng; vai trò; ảnh hưởng

g: noun — a motto, a maxim one lives by; a guiding saying kept close at hand
gv: danh từ — câu châm ngôn để sống theo; câu nói dẫn đường luôn để bên mình

g: verb — to fight, to wage war, to do battle
gv: động từ — chiến đấu, đánh trận

g: noun — author, writer; the one who made the work
gv: danh từ — tác giả, người viết; người làm ra tác phẩm

g: verb — to oversee in person; to be in command on the spot
gv: động từ — đích thân ngồi trông coi; trực tiếp chỉ huy tại chỗ

g: verb — to testify, to bear witness, to give evidence
gv: động từ — làm chứng, đưa ra bằng chứng

g: verb — to testify; to bear witness; to serve as evidence
gv: động từ — làm chứng; đứng ra làm chứng; làm bằng chứng

g: Doubles a one-syllable quality word and pins it with 的 onto a noun, painting the quality as a lived, dwelt-on image.
Coverb that names a rule, plan, or instruction the following action is carried out to match.
Disposal of a definite object where the verb doubles (V一V / VV), making the action a brief, light pass rather than a finished result.
Front a definite object with 把, act on it with a designating verb (定/列/視/稱/譯), and cap that verb with 為 + a noun naming the formal designation now assigned to it.
Front a definite thing with 把, then 看/當/叫 + 作/做 names the new category you assign it to.
Front a definite object with 把, act on it, and cap the verb with 成 + a noun naming the new thing the object has turned into.
Takes a definite object, moves it, and lands it at a named place; 來 if the place is toward the speaker, 去 if away.
Fronts a definite object with 把, then disposes of it with a verb closed by 了: the thing gets dealt with and is gone or used up.
Front a definite object with 把, act on it with the verb, and let 在 + place name where the object comes to rest.
Front a definite object with 把, then a verb that must carry a result complement saying the new state the object ends in.
Take a definite thing, do the verb to it, and 給 closes the verb by landing it with a receiver.
Marks disposal: take a definite object and say what the action does to it.
States a percentage: 百分之 holds the hundred fixed as the base, and the number after 之 says how many of those hundred parts are meant — 百分之五 = five percent.
Benefactive: do the action for someone, in their place.
Passive whose verb carries a 得 complement: the subject takes the action, and 得 opens onto the knock-on state it was driven to.
Passive whose verb carries a 成 result: the subject takes the action, and 成 marks the new state it was turned into.
Passive: an agent treats or regards the subject AS something else, pinning an identity on them.
Agentless passive: the affected thing is the subject; 被 sits before the verb with no doer named.
Multiplicative comparison: states A as N times the amount of baseline B.
Passive: the subject is the one acted upon by an agent.
Marks the original state of affairs as a starting point that something later moved away from.
Comparison that reports the result: A turns out to hold more of the quality than B; 來得 sits before the quality and frames it as the verdict that comes out.
Comparison: ranks A above B on one quality; A holds more of it.
Comparison: A measured against B on some quality.
Negative comparison: A cannot measure up to B; B is the better one, A the lesser. Built as a potential complement — 比 (match) cannot reach 上 (level with).
Tells the listener to break off an action already in progress; 再 adds "any more," 了 marks the turn to the new state.
Tells the listener not to do an action; 請 in front bows the command into a polite request.
Concession: raises a true surface fact only to wave it off, because the conclusion it seems to invite does not hold; the second clause turns the other way.
Concede a strong case with 別說, then top it: 連…也 names an even more surprising one that still holds.
Sets aside the surface considerations and appeals to one underlying fact that settles the matter.
Sets 並 before a negation to deny an assumption the listener is expected to hold: contrary to what you'd think, not so.
Auxiliary before a verb: marks the action as required with no alternative — must, have to.
Conditional negation: a withheld first action gates a second; as long as the first does not happen, the second cannot either.
Negative condition: A is left undone or absent, and then the consequence fails to follow.
Ask a yes-no question that leans toward a negative guess: 不 or 沒 negates the verb, 嗎 closes it, and the speaker checks a hunch drawn from what they see.
Double-negation modal: negating a modal of possibility/permission forces the action — "cannot not do it", so it must be done.

Sets 不 before a degree word (太, 很) so the negation lands on the amount, not the quality — it falls short of the mark rather than flatly failing. 不太 is the everyday softener; 不很 is rarer and confined to set contexts.
Denies a state, a quality, an intention, or a habit — present, general, or future. (For past completion, use 沒.)
Tells the listener to stop or not do an action; 請 in front softens the command to a polite request.
Denies the expected outcome with 不/沒, then 反而 supplies its reverse — the opposite of what was looked for; 不但 may front the denial to play up the surprise.
Grants one claim about a subject, then adds a second that goes further on the same subject.
Stacks a second fact on top of the first about the same subject — not only A, but also B; the two add up rather than clash.
Set before a quantity to report it falls short of that figure — under, fewer than, less than the mark named.
Placed before a verb, marks the action as forbidden by a rule or standing prohibition — may not, must not.
No matter how the open clause turns out, the same outcome holds across every value of it.
Denies that a conclusion necessarily follows: the expected result is not guaranteed, though it may still hold.
Introduces an outcome that contradicts what was reckoned beforehand; the speaker had measured the situation one way, and reality broke that forecast.
Whichever of the listed options holds, the same outcome follows across all of them.
Marks an outcome as unavoidable given the situation: the following state or reaction is bound to come, one can't help it.
Comparison: A comes up short against B; B is the better one, A the lesser.
Negates a wrong account and substitutes the correct one in its place: not X, but rather Y.

Sets 不是 in front of a degree-marked quality (很 + stative) to deny that it reaches the high mark, leaving the quality in a soft middle. Reads as a tentative, face-saving 'not entirely / not quite', gentler than docking the degree outright.
Exhaustive alternation: rule out the first option and the second is what stands; it is always one of the two.
Frames a statement the speaker holds to be true as a negated question, pressing the listener to grant the obvious positive.
Stacks two merits: 不說 waves the first off as already granted, 還 lands a further, weightier one on top.
Cancels a requirement before a verb: the action was expected or needed, and now it no longer is. 不用 and 不必 are interchangeable here.
Marks a state or habit that used to hold and has now ended: 不 forbids the next repetition, 再 is that repetition, 了 closes the door behind into the new situation.
Grants a first quality or member, then adds a further one that goes beyond it; the two stack on the same subject.
Marks the speaker's own uncertainty about an embedded question, with no answer asked of the listener.
Denies that things go so far as the bad or extreme case named after it: it may be heading that way, but it does not reach that point.
Sits before the verb to drive the speaker's own stance down hard against what was assumed or said, frequently sealed by 呢 at the end.
The first event has barely begun when the second already follows: the short gap reads as sooner than expected.
Adverb before the verb: the action has only just happened, barely a moment back, with the next event riding in right on its heels.
Adverb before the verb: the action holds back until a prior event or condition is met, and arrives only then, later than a listener would expect.
Marks the second clause as reached only after the first is met: the result is later, harder, or smaller than one might expect.
Sits before a quantity to mark it as small, low, or short of what was expected: only, merely.
An outcome came within a hair of happening; the gap left was one small point and it did not (or only barely) tip over.
Says the gap left is not much: two things come out about the same, or an amount has almost reached its mark.
Acts inside a window that is still open: do the main thing while the temporary condition holds, before it closes.
Directional: 出 fixed after a verb carries the action outward, from inside an enclosure to outside it.
Figurative: 出 fixed after a verb marks that the action brings something forth — a thought, a finding, a product — into being or into view where there was nothing before.
Besides this: 此 seals off everything just said, and the clause that follows adds something further beyond it.
Marks the one and only condition (除非 X) under which an outcome holds; 否則 carries the default that takes over whenever that condition is not met.
Except: one item is set aside, and 都 covers all the rest under one predicate.
Besides the named item, the count keeps going: set X aside, and there is still more.
Apart from: one thing is fenced off after 除了…之外, and the second clause speaks of everything beyond it.
Treat the prior point as closed, point back at it, then introduce a separate further point or item from beyond it; written-register 'besides this'.
Marks a span: 從 sets where it starts, 到 sets where it ends — across place, time, or range.
Fronts one chosen vantage or party, then carries out a deliberate action shaped by that vantage.
Fixes a point (usually a time) as the origin and projects what follows forward from it: from that point on, the rule or state holds.
From: marks the place a motion sets out from.
Marks the point an action sets out from (a time, place, or step); the action runs forward from there, with no endpoint fixed.
Fixes the starting point with 從, then sets the heading with 往, and a motion verb travels along it: leave from here, aim that way, go.
Marks a span: 從 fixes the starting point, 到 fixes the ending point, and the stretch between is the whole range.
Sweeps the entire span of past time up to now: across all of it, not once — 不 for a standing habit (never does), 沒 for completion (never has, paired with 過).
Wraps a clause into a span of time and stamps the main action as happening within that span: when / while X holds, Y.
Keep the prior clause as given, then state one thing that runs against it.
Tails a verb with the point the action runs up to and stops: a time reached, a place reached, an amount reached.
Tails a verb to report the action succeeded — the thing was reached, found, gotten, perceived.
Sends the subject to a place, then states what they do there: 到 + place + 去/來 + verb phrase.

Clause-internal adverb marking the predicate as a mild turn against what the first clause led you to expect — concedes the setup, then leans the other way (actually / on the contrary / as it happens). 是 commonly attaches as 倒是.
Sets a range between two endpoints: the near end is named, 到 is laid down, then the far end, and the whole stretch between them is taken in.
Attributive: binds a modifier to the noun it describes.
Degree complement: rates how the action turned out.
Omission of 的: the linker is dropped when the bond is close — kin and one's own institutions. 我媽媽, 我們學校, 你朋友.
A whole verb phrase or clause sits before 的 with no head noun behind it; 的 then names the thing the clause is about — what it does, makes, or discusses.
Tags the clause before it as a supposition; the consequence follows, often with 就.
Manner/degree complement: the action happens, then 得 opens onto a verdict on how it went.
Pins a quality word in front of a verb as the manner the action is carried out.
The noun after 的 is left out when context already names it; 的 then stands for it: 藍的 = the blue one.
Possessive: A 的 B marks B as belonging to A.
Attributive: a whole verb phrase sits before 的 to describe the noun behind it — the N that does VP.
A quality word (stative verb) sits before a noun to name it by that quality, with 的 as the linker between them.
Deixis along one line: 這 points near (this), 那 points far (that), 哪 marks the spot you point at without knowing which (asks: which one).
Sits after a short list to close it as open — these are samples, more of the same kind follow unnamed.
Turns a count into a rank: 第 in front of a number names which one in the sequence, not how many.
Resultative: 掉 fixed after a verb marks that the object falls away — used up, disposed of, or gone.
Frozen A-不-A adverb: at the least stir, the consequence fires right away, and keeps firing — the speaker holds the trigger too slight.
Double a two-syllable verb whole (AB to ABAB) to make the action light, brief, and low-stakes. It frames a quick go rather than the full deed.
All: one predicate covers every member of the set named before it.
Already, by now: 都 before a count, span, or stage and 了 at the end says the tally has climbed this high, with the weight that it is high enough to expect things had moved on.
Frames a perspective-holder, then states how something weighs or appears from their standpoint.
Frames a perspective-holder, then states how something weighs or appears from their standpoint, in a formal register.
Marks the person an action faces: 對 names who the action is aimed at, the one who receives the facing.
Front 對 to aim at a target, then say 有興趣 (have interest) about it: be interested in X.
Set 多 or 少 before the verb to do more or less of the action.
Front a stative verb with 多 and close with 啊 to exclaim how far a quality reaches — how pretty, how big, how good it is.
Put 多 before a stative verb (高/大/重) to ask how far up that quality reaches.
Set 多少 before the verb to assert that the action happened to some degree — an amount left open but not zero.
Join two balanced clauses where the second runs counter to the first, stating both as parallel facts.
Keep the prior clause as given, then stack a second point that runs the same way and reinforces it.
Caps a clause at the very end to shrink it to no more than what was said: merely this, and that is all.
Sweeps aside the options or doubts just raised and asserts a conclusion that holds no matter which one is true.
Opens a second clause that states the opposite case to the one just made — flip the prior condition, get the parallel result.
Two-part necessity frame: 非 rules out all options but the bracketed action, 不(可) bars leaving it undone — so the action must be done, there is no alternative.
Pushes past expectation: not only does A hold, B carries the same direction further, often downhill — the slide that was supposed to stop instead keeps going.
States a fraction: the whole is split into [denominator] equal parts, of which [numerator] are taken — total parts named before the part taken.
States the proposition first, then warns of the consequence that follows if it does not hold: do this, or else that happens.
Auxiliary before a verb: marks the action as the fitting thing for the subject to do (should, ought to) — the bare, condensed form of 應該, often negated as 不該 (ought not).
Auxiliary before a verb, the spoken short form of 應該: the situation has come due and the action now falls to the subject — it's my turn / time to do it. Often closed with 了.
Approximate count: 幾 takes a digit's place to mean an unfixed few — 幾個 a few, 十幾 -teen, 幾十 dozens.
Marks that a cause produces an outcome and delivers it to a specified recipient: [cause] 給 [recipient] 帶來 [outcome].
Hand a thing to a receiver: 給 takes the person who gets it, then the thing they get.
Sets a reference point as the backdrop, then makes a claim about something measured against it.
States that one thing is tied to another (有關) or cut loose from it (沒…關係); 跟 names the thing it is measured against.
Parity comparison: A and B come out about even on a measurable dimension, close enough to count as matched.
Equal comparison: A matches B, with no gap on the quality named at the tail.
Marks the person spoken to: subject says or tells something to that listener.
With: links the subject to a second person, and the two share one action.
With / follow: do something alongside or after someone.
Adverb before the verb meaning at root; paired with a negator (沒/不) it denies the action all the way down — not at all, not in the slightest.
Grants a mild case to make an extreme case follow without stating it: X already holds at the weak end, so it holds all the more at the named harder end.
Grants a case where something already holds, then names a second case where it holds even more plainly, so that one argues itself.
Names the source a following statement rests on; the claim is presented as grounded in it.
Verb suffix: the action has been done so often it has settled into habit and now feels natural.
A verb clipped to 光 says the supply was used right down to nothing — bare, none left.
Frame: 光 strips the input down to one bare source or action, and 就 fires the result off that alone — nothing else was needed.
Coverb that names the subject a following piece of discourse or a noun is about.
Completive marker: within a recent bounded span (today, this morning), the verb's action has been gone through and is finished, so it need not be done again now.
Experiential marker: the verb's action has been lived through at least once before now.
Experiential: the action has been done at least once before.
After a verb of changing or fixing, 過來 turns something off-track back to its correct, normal, right-facing state.
After a motion verb, 過 carries it across a stretch, and 來/去 set whether it crosses toward the speaker or away.
Marks an outcome that arrives exactly as foreseen; the speaker predicted or suspected it, and the event confirms the prediction.
Marks a further activity the same subject takes on in addition to what was just stated — and on top of that, also does B.
Marks an action or state as not done up to now, and still pending: 還 holds the not-yet open and points it forward to an expected change.
Sits before an evaluation to soften it to a passing grade: the thing is acceptable, good enough, better than its low billing — without being outright good.
Inside a 比 comparison, 還 pushes the scale one step past a baseline already high: A is even more X than B.
Links a culprit's (usually careless) action to the bad outcome it inflicts on someone else, with the blame riding along.
After weighing the situation, settle back on the better course and nudge the listener toward it gently.
Choice question: lay out two named options and ask the listener to pick one.
And: sets two noun-like items side by side as one equal pair.
With: place a second person beside the subject so they share the same activity.
Rates how easy or hard the action is to carry out: 好 in front of the verb says easy, 難 says hard.
Pushes a vague quantity higher: 好 prepends to 幾 / 多 / 些 before a measure word and reads the loose count as a sizeable one — quite a few, a good many.
Joins two clauses so the first does the groundwork and 好 opens onto the second: the thing the first clears the way for, now able to happen.
Resultative complement: the verb runs until the thing is finished well and ready for use, not merely stopped.
Tag a suggestion at the tail and ask the listener to pick between yes and no: ..., good or not?
Tag a suggestion or request, then ask the listener to agree: ..., is that good?
Names the one good fact that the situation rests on, the thing that keeps the trouble from mattering.
Restates the fact just given in different wording: the same point, said another way.
After granting one line of thought, turns the talk back to raise a balancing or contrary consideration on the other side.
Set a bare item out front as what the sentence is about, mark it off with a pause, then make a self-standing remark inside that frame.
Auxiliary before a verb: have the learned skill to do it, picked up by training or practice.
Wraps a forecast and closes it as a settled certainty: it surely will.
Predicts that an event is likely to come about — will, is going to, would (probably). Set before the verb to forecast the outcome, not the speaker's plan.
Two question words for quantity: 幾 asks a small countable number and clutches a measure word; 多少 asks an open amount and needs none.
Attach 幾 to a round number to mean that number plus an unspecified few; its position fixes the size. 幾 after a round number (十幾) adds a few units on top; 幾 before 十/百 (幾十) counts a few of that rung.
Stacks two facts that hold of one subject at once: 既 sets the first as a given, 也 adds the second alongside it.
Pairs two qualities of one subject, the second resting on the same footing the first sets — a balanced, matched pair held together.
Resultative on a perception verb: the looking or listening made contact and the percept registered.
Pulls a definite object ahead of the verb so the verb acts on it and resolves it — disposal in formal, written register.
In brief: closes off what was just said and restates it in one compressed line.
Marks an exaggerated verdict: drop every qualification and treat the claim as effectively reaching its extreme — it as good as IS so.
Pivotal verb: subject tells or gets a named person to perform the following action; that person is both the one called and the one who acts.
A names a person and the action that person is to carry out: A 叫 [someone] [verb phrase] — the someone is the object of 叫 and the doer of the verb at once.
Conditional: entertains a case as provisional, then states what follows once it holds; 就 marks the consequence and may drop.
Sentence-initial framing adverbial: marks what follows as true at the core of the matter, holding in the main while leaving room for exceptions on top.
Resultative: a verb fused to the outcome it produced.
Heads the clause reporting what a run of events finally came to, often a turn against what was set up.
Adverb before the verb that flags the outcome as running against expectation.
Takes a span of time or a process and presents the whole of it as traversed end-to-end; the result clause states what holds once that span has been passed through in full.
Sits before the verb to mark the outcome as a surprise, clashing with what the prior context led you to expect.
Concession: grant a situation at its fullest extent, then hold the far clause regardless.
Names the route or process a thing was arrived at through, then states that the thing came about that way; 經由 opens the means, 而來 marks the thing as its arrival rather than a separate consequence.
Draws a conclusion from a premise already taken as true: given that A holds, then B follows.
Grant the most extreme or unlikely case, and the outcome in the second clause holds anyway.
Frames one named respect or aspect, then states a fact that holds only within that respect, leaving other respects untouched.
Adverb before the verb on a singled-out subject: a general claim is laid down, and 就 points to this one case that runs against it.
Adverb before the verb: the action lands promptly, right on the back of the first event or condition.
Adverb before the verb: a small or single slice of the whole already tallies a count higher than a listener would expect.
Adverbial 就是 before a predicate marks it as holding firm against everything raised against it — simply, no matter what, it just is so.
Wraps a verb to mark it as about to happen at any moment, with 了 signalling the new state about to land.
Concessive conditional: grant even the most extreme supposed case, and the outcome still holds.
Concessive conditional: grant the worst case as settled, and the result holds anyway.
Sets 絕 before 不 to drive a negation to its limit: under no circumstances, with no exception left standing.
Pre-verbal stance adverb marking the stated fact as true and confirmed yet running against everything the buildup predicted — astonishment that it is so.
Sentence-front phrase marking the whole statement as secondhand report — the speaker passes it on without vouching for it.
Begin an activity: 開始 opens onto the verb that names what starts.
Withholds an answer until a named factor decides it: the outcome depends on X. 要/得 add that you have to consult that factor first.
Verb doubled and capped with 看: do the action briefly as a trial and see how it comes out.
Reads a visible sign and states the likely conclusion drawn from it: by the look of things, it seems that…
Coverb naming the support a result is gotten through: you put your weight on X to bring the action off; 著 trails it when the leaning is an ongoing habit.
Sits directly before a single bare verb in written register: the action is possible or worth doing, and the verb is read in the passive-potential — can be done, is -able.
Bare 可 set straight before a verb in written register: doing the fronted thing serves to bring about the good outcome; the compressed, formal form of 可以.
Set 可 before the predicate to press the speaker's commitment onto the whole assertion, marking the claim as one to take seriously and weighting it above a flat report; often rides a sentence-final 了.
Sits before a verb to mark the act as permitted — may, be allowed to. The bare single-character form belongs to formal and written register, and most often appears negated as 不可 (may not, must not).
A fixed three-character reply that echoes the previous speaker's claim back as emphatic agreement: a negative-question shell, "isn't that so", landing as a heavier yes.
Connective opening the second clause: from what was just stated, the following conclusion can be seen.
Potential: whether the verb can reach its result.
Auxiliary before a verb: the action is sanctioned — someone permits it or the rules leave it open. Negated (不可以), permission is withheld.
Before a verb whose subject is an activity or condition: doing this brings a benefit within reach — it can serve to, it does you good.
Grants or withholds leave to do something: you may, you are allowed to.
An event is on the verge of happening; it will arrive very soon.
After the verb, 來 sends the motion toward where the speaker stands; 去 sends it away.
Sentence-initial marker that flags the statement to follow as the speaker's unvarnished view, often something blunt or unwelcome.
Verb-suffix le: marks the verb's action as carried through to completion.
Change of state: a new situation now holds that did not before.
Completed action: marks the verb's action as realized.
Sequence: once the first action is finished, the next follows right after.
Reports that a quality has already shifted by a small amount: it has gotten a bit more X than before.
A verb or adjective plus sentence-final 了 reports that a new condition now holds that did not hold before.
States how far apart two points are: A sits some distance from the reference point B.
Measures the span to a coming point in time: 離 fixes a future event, and what follows says how much time is still left before it.
Even: the hardest case is included, so the rest follow.
Measure words: a counter chosen to fit the noun's shape or class.

Double a single-syllable measure word (朵 to 朵朵, 個 to 個個) to sweep across every member of its set: each and all, no exception. It runs the counter over the whole group and leaves none out, usually with 都.
Zero pronoun: leave the subject slot empty when the doer is already named and unchanged.
A quality or situation makes a person feel a reaction; the cause comes first, 令 hands the feeling down onto the person named after.
Treat the prior point as closed, then introduce a separate further item or remark that stands on its own.
Location order: an address names the largest container first and narrows to the exact spot, running country, city, road, number.
Yes-no question: turns a statement into a question.
Resultative on a verb of putting-in or covering: the action filled the space to the brim, with no room left.
Denies that something is held or is there: 沒(有) bars possession and existence. (For will, quality, or habit, use 不.)
Sentence-final 沒（有） that turns a completed/experiential statement into a yes-no question by appending the negative half: did it happen or not?
Pins one thing's existence to another: take the first away and the second is gone too. The whole frame credits Y to X by sinking both.
Negative comparison: A falls short of B on quality X; A is not as X as B. 這/那麼 names the degree B already holds.
Universal claim by double negation: not one single case fails the predicate, so every case meets it without exception.
Suffix on a noun for people; marks the referent as a group rather than one.
Marks an unwanted outcome the first action is taken to head off; you act now so that the bad thing never comes to pass.
Sits before the verb to assert a fact as plainly, evidently the case, setting it against a later move that ignores or contradicts it.
Picks up a concrete item and brings it to bear: take X and put it to use as material, instrument, or example for what follows.
Pulls out one specific case as a representative example to back up a general claim.
Question word for a place. 哪裡 holds the slot where the location answer will go: where. It stays in that place slot, often after locative 在 (在哪裡).
Discourse connective: take what was just said as given, then draw the next move from it — in that case, then.

哪裡 set in front of a verb of knowing or expecting denies that the knowing ever happened, and flags the clause that follows as a turn no one saw coming: how was I to know that…; who would have guessed that….
Opens the consequence: takes a situation already on the table and points to what follows from it — in that case, then.

難道 opens a question the speaker holds up only to knock down: the surface asks one thing, the speaker means its flat opposite, and 嗎 closes the dare at the end. A surface negative means an emphatic yes; a surface positive means an emphatic no.
Marks that a previously puzzling fact has stopped being puzzling now that its cause is known: 'no wonder', 'that explains it'.
Marks an outcome as hard to avoid given the situation: the following state or reaction is bound to come, though it is never anyone's fault.
Sentence-final particle: names a new topic and hands the previous predicate to it, so the predicate is left unsaid.
Sentence-final particle: sits on an already-formed question (usually a wh-question) and holds it open, drawing it out into a softer, musing inquiry.
Sits before a verb to assert the subject has the inherent capacity, room, or conditions to do it. (Learned skill is 會; permission is 可以.)
Says the conditions allow the action — free to, in a position to. Negated (不能), the conditions block it.
Opener that turns the listener's own eye onto the situation and invites the conclusion that sight delivers: look, see for yourself.

Marks a willing trade-off: the speaker would sooner take the lesser bad named after 寧可 (paying its cost) than let the worse thing happen. 也要 holds onto what is worth the cost; 也不要 refuses the worse thing at any cost.
Costed preference: accept the hard option in the first clause to protect what you will or will not do in the second.
Opener that lays a situation before the listener and puts the judgment of it in their mouth — you tell me, wouldn't you say.
Counting frame: a number cannot touch a noun directly — a measure word sits between them. [number] [measure] [noun].
Number naming: each digit is said with its place-value word (十 百 千 萬); an empty middle place is held open with 零.
Coverb naming the backing a result is gotten through — the strength, grounds, or credential you draw on; it fronts before the verb, where it carries the outcome.
Existential: a place leads, a verb follows, and the last slot introduces a new, indefinite thing as existing, appearing, or gone at that place.
Marks whether you can bear the cost the verb demands: V得起 can afford/sustain it, V不起 cannot.
After a verb, 起 lifts the object up off the surface it rested on, often into the hand.
Presses the speaker's full weight onto an instruction the listener must not get wrong; sits before 要 or 別/不要/不能.
After a verb, 起來 sends the action upward — off the ground, up to standing or held aloft.
After a verb, 起來 marks the onset: the action sets off from a standstill and keeps going.
After a verb of gathering or shutting, 起來 stows the thing away into a closed, put-aside state — collected, packed, hidden, or locked.
After a verb of perceiving or trying (看, 聽, 說, 吃, 做), 起來 turns it into a verdict: judged through that channel, it comes across as X.
A verb phrase with the subject dropped stands alone as a command aimed at the listener; 請 in front bows the order into a request.
Fronts a verb to ask someone to do it, softening a command into a polite request.
A fixed opener set in front of a question to a stranger: it marks the asker as deferring before asking, and leaves the question's own structure untouched.
adv — sets aside the impression just raised and states what is really the case.
Names a part inside a group already on the table: of that set, this one.
Fixed after a verb, 去 says the action carries its object off — out of a place or off a set, so the object is no longer there.
Clause-internal adverb marking the predicate as running counter to what the first clause led you to expect; sits after the subject, before the verb.
Directional: shows which way the action travels.
Free choice: the same question word appears on both sides of 就; the first slot picks freely, and the second is whatever the first turned out to be.
Sentence-initial conjunction: concedes the prior statement as true, then pivots to a contrasting point — however, yet.
A grants or causes the room for B to do the verb: A 讓 B V — the action belongs to B, set off by A.
Permissive causative: 讓 takes the person, then the verb they are allowed to do. The subject grants it; the person performs it.
Joins two actions in order: the first finishes, then the next follows.
Double a quality word and close it with 的 to make a vivid, lingering description of how something is.
Any at all: the noun is opened to every member of its kind, none singled out, none excluded. Positive sentences usually pair it with a following 都; negative ones read as not a single one.
Takes the situation just stated as a given and projects the consequence it now makes possible; the written-register form of 這樣一來.
Conditional: a condition, then the consequence once it is met.
Opens a supposed clause: set a condition that has not happened, then say what would follow from it.
Sentence-final tag that lays out a proposal in full, then hands it to the listener for a verdict — how about it, what do you say.
Single-character literary 'if': opens a supposed case in terse written register, then states what would follow; needs no 就 pickup.
Directional: 上 fixed after a verb sends the action upward, or brings it into contact onto a surface.
Opens with a rhetorical who-made-you that pins a bad outcome on the listener's own earlier action; no answer is wanted.
Question word for a person: 誰 holds the slot where the named person will go — who.
A rhetorical "who knew" set before an event that already happened, framing it as something nobody could have foreseen.
Question word for an open thing. 什麼 holds an empty slot where the answer noun will go and names the thing being asked for.
Everything except one: 什麼都 sweeps the whole set under one predicate; 就是 carves out the single case that breaks it.
Pushes a statement to its farthest case: even this, the one you would least expect, holds too.
A formal frame that holds a decision or value open and lets it be fixed by a named factor: it is decided according to X. The factor sits inside, between 視 and 而定.
A cause makes a person or thing end up in a new state: [cause] 使 [target] [resulting state].
Copula: sets two nouns equal — X is Y. Links a noun to a noun for identity or category, never a noun to an adjective.
Stressed 是 before a whole predicate (often an adjective), granting or affirming that the claim holds true; bare 很 would merely state it.
Focus: foregrounds one detail (time, place, manner, agent) of a known past event.
Question a whole proposition: set 是 (so) and 不是 (not so) side by side before the verb phrase, asking the listener to confirm the guess.
A cause clause comes first, then 使得 hands its outcome forward: what follows is the resulting state the cause brings about.
Measures how long an action has been absent: the duration sits before the negated verb and counts the empty stretch, not the doing. Final 了 stretches the gap up to now.
Folds a yes-no question into a statement as an embedded "whether (or not)" clause; the proposition follows 是否.
Duration complement: a time-amount sits after the verb to say how long the action has run.
adv — sets aside what was assumed or popularly held and plants the sentence on what the facts actually show.
Affected-by: the topic comes first as the thing borne upon; 受 takes a noun factor, then a verb naming the influence it exerts.
Approximate excess: 多 wedges in after a round number to add an unstated remainder on top of it — over X, X-and-a-bit.
Move by tracing an existing line — a road, an edge, a sound, a current; 順 takes the line, 著 holds the motion bound to it the whole way.
One verb is echoed on both sides, and the thing happens the instant it is raised, with no interval and no warning.
Front a speaking-verb plus 到 with a topic to mark it as the thing now under discussion, then make the remark about it.
A state word pushed to its extreme: 死了 hangs on the back of an adjective to mean that state taken all the way, past the limit.
Says a sum aloud: first number, the operation verb (加 add / 減 subtract / 乘以 times / 除以 divided by), second number, 等於 equals, then the result.
Weighs a thing and judges it falls inside a category — counts as, qualifies as. The optional 是 pins it to the category named.
Closes a matter the speaker chooses to stop pursuing: drop it, forget it, never mind.
Concession: set up an expectation, then turn against it.
Concession: grant one thing, then pivot to what actually holds; the granting clause can drop, the pivot cannot.
Coverb that names an unfolding process or change as the driver; the main clause states what shifts along with it.
所 in front of a transitive verb turns the action into its object — the thing V acts on — and 的 hands that thing to a noun or stands in for it.
Names a term with 所謂, then spells out what that term means with 是指 or 就是: the so-called X is/refers to Y.
A reactive opener: the speaker takes what the other just said and lands the conclusion it was always pointing to, with a knowing shrug.
Marks a quality as past the acceptable limit, with 了 closing the verdict.
Benefactive: do the action standing in someone's place, on their behalf.
Time runs largest unit first: year, then month, then day; hour, then minute.
Marks the named process or channel by which a result was reached: through doing X, Y came about.
Marks the medium, lens, or channel passed through to reach or perceive what lies on the far side: through X, Y is reached.
Post-verb place complement: 到 sends the verb to an endpoint it reaches; 在 leaves the verb settled at the spot it lands on.
Potential of figurative 出來: whether the verb can bring something hidden out into recognition — 得出來 makes it out, 不出來 cannot.
Whether the action has the force to make the object move or shift.
Wedges 得 or 不 between a verb and its result to say whether the result can be reached.
Potential where the result is 來: whether the doing comes off and clicks into place — 得來 it works out, 不來 it never takes.
Potential: whether the verb can be carried through to the full amount — 得了 manages it, 不了 falls short of finishing.
Potential: whether the verb gets the object down into a space that holds it — fits or doesn't fit.
A verb plus 個 plus a tail that refuses to close (不停 won't stop / 沒完 no end); the action goes on and on without letting up.
Do the verb to the full, until you have had your fill of it.
After the verb, a path word (進/出/上/回) names which way the action moves through space, then 來 or 去 anchors that path to the speaker: 來 heads toward where the speaker stands, 去 heads away.
Doubles the verb to make the action a single brief, light pass; 一 counts it as one go, 了 between the halves marks that one pass done.
Threads an object through 起…來 to mark taking up an activity — the action sets off and runs on, often catching the listener off guard.
A verb fused to a state word: the action runs, and the state names what it leaves behind.
Suffixes 於 onto a verb to fasten the action to the point it turns on — the place it lands at, the source it issues from, the time or domain it sits in.
One subject runs two verb phrases back to back, in the order they happen: the first act, then what it is for or what follows.
Tags a comparison: the gap on quality Vs is large — A is much more X than B.
Hangs the frozen verdict 沒話說 behind a quality: it is so far past good that there is nothing left to say.
Degree complement: 得 hinges a state word onto 要命, pushing that state to where it feels unbearable.
Attaches 壞了 to a feeling-verb to push the state to its unbearable limit, the body or mood at breaking point.
Three tails that clamp behind a stative verb to push its degree to the extreme.
An action held open and repeated runs on, and somewhere inside that stretch an unplanned new state arrives unnoticed.
A verb clipped to 完 says the action was carried right through to its end, nothing of it left to do.
Flags a remote, undesirable contingency and pairs it with a precaution: in the unlikely event that X, then Y.
Benefactive: names the person the action is done for, who gains from it; the verb phrase follows.
Negates the experiential: states an event has not once happened up to now; formal counterpart of 從來沒…過.
Marks a deliberate action B as undertaken for the sake of purpose A; the goal is named first, the action that serves it follows after 而.
Names the goal an action is aimed at; the purpose is stated, then the action taken to reach it.
Question word asking for the reason or purpose behind an action or state: why.
Fronts a clause to mark what follows as the speaker's own read of the situation — a sized-up opinion or piece of advice, softened by being owned.
Bars an action in formal or written notice: do not, must not; 請 prepended softens the ban to a courteous request.
Marks the verb after it as impossible for the subject to carry out; sits before a verb to say there is no way to do it.
Whatever way things turn out and by whatever means, the stated outcome stands — the situation is sealed shut into the fixed phrase 如何, so no case escapes it.
States that the alternatives that follow carry no weight — either is acceptable, the choice makes no difference, the speaker does not mind.
adv — cancels an obligation before a verb: there is no need to do it
Figurative: 下 fixed after a verb of getting or fixing marks that the thing is brought down into one's own possession or record and held there — bought, kept, written, taken in.
Directional: 下 fixed after a verb sends the action downward and lets it settle into place, ending in rest.
Tags a verb's motion as downward, then splits on who it ends near: 下來 brings it down toward the speaker, 下去 carries it down away from them.
Orders two actions in one subject's plan: the 先 action is done first, the 再 action follows after it.
Brackets an open sample list: 像 opens with 'such as', the named items sit between, and 什麼的 trails to leave the rest of the kind unnamed.
Auxiliary before a verb: a wish to do the action, held in mind without being committed to yet.
Likeness: A resembles B, behaving or appearing in the same manner as B; the shared trait sits at the tail.
Toward: orients the action at a target.
Opens a clause to mark it as moving in matched proportion to the change just stated; correspondingly, by the same token.
Opens a clause that runs the opposite way to the one just stated, naming the contrary course.
The decimal point is said 點; the whole part before it is read with place-values, the fraction after it digit by digit.
Range span: sets one end at the trivial case and the other at the major case, sweeping in everything between.
Tagged after a verb, it carries the action on from where it stands — keep doing it, let it run forward without stopping.
Marks the lucky circumstance that cut a looming bad outcome short before it landed.
Marks the line or edge that a motion keeps to for its whole length — moving along (a river, a wall, a road), not toward an endpoint.
Auxiliary before a verb: a settled plan to do the action soon.
Auxiliary before a verb: marks the action as required of the subject — must, need to, have to.
Sets a verb as the subject's settled intention, leaning into about-to when 了 closes the clause.
Names the unwanted consequence that follows if the first clause is not acted on: do X, or else Y happens.
Counterfactual: names something that did happen, supposes it had not, and credits the real outcome to it.
Conditional: supposes a case, then states what follows once it holds.
Restates the clause just made as its plain equivalent or upshot: the same fact said again, so that is to say.
Also: the new predicate holds the same as one already said.
Lays two parallel things one subject does or can do side by side; each is marked 也, the first one often dropped.
Distributive progression: one counted unit, then the next, the action advancing one at a time.
The instant a verb of talking or recalling raises a subject into view, a consequence follows: once one gets onto the subject of X, this is what happens.
Frames a chosen criterion or angle, then reports how the matter looks or rates when sighted along it.
Emphatic total negation: not even the single smallest case holds, so none of the set does.
Take a named instance X and put it forward as the example that backs a general claim.
Frame that ranks one element of a set as the dominant one, the rest subordinate: take X and make it the main thing.
Step escalation: each successive unit outdoes the one before it on a quality, climbing steadily.
Immediacy: the instant the first happens, the second follows.
Coverb that names the means, method, or channel an action is carried out through; formal counterpart of 用.
Joins forward from an action just stated to the goal that action serves; written register, sits at the comma after the measure, before the purpose clause.

Takes a physical line, road, or landmark as the reference, then names which side of it is meant: 以內 the side inside it, 以外 the side past it, 以東/以西/以南/以北 the compass side of it, 以左/以右 the side to its left or right.
Sets a quantity as a boundary marker, then names which direction the open range runs from it: 以上 that figure and up, 以下 that figure and down, 以內 inside the limit, 以外 past it.
Pick up a thing or means with 以 (X), then cap it with 為 + a noun (Y) that names the role X is made to serve: take X as Y, regard X as Y, use X as Y — formal written register.
Sentence-initial framing adverbial of formal or written register: marks what follows as the broad general case, holding for most instances and leaving room for exceptions.
Sentence-initial framing adverbial: marks what follows as the broad general case, true of most instances, leaving room for exceptions.
Runs two actions on parallel tracks at the same time: one 一邊 opens each track, both verbs in progress together.
Once a hypothetical event breaks, the consequence follows and is hard to take back; the second clause often carries 就.
Raises a stative verb by a small amount: a bit more X.
Emphatic total negation: not even the smallest amount, so none at all.
Holds two aspects of one matter level at once: each 一方面 turns the same thing to show one face, both true together and both to be weighed.
Postposed time-relator: marks everything downstream of the point named before it.
Splits one short continuous stretch into quick-swapping moments: one instant it is the first, the next instant the second, switching back and forth fast.
And: caps a list by reaching out to one more item, often the longest or the one that matters most, in formal or written register.
Adverbial before the verb: the whole action runs through without a break, often covering a large quantity.
Counts off grounds for one conclusion in order: the first reason, then the second, both pointing at the same verdict.
Runs two actions of one subject together in the same stretch of time: each 一面 opens one of them, both verbs in progress at once.
One clause holds both a cause and its result: 因(為) marks what brings it about, 而 carries straight on to what comes of it.
Therefore: a fact already stated is taken as the cause, and the consequence follows from it.
Bounds a measured span of time or quantity, placing the event anywhere inside that limit, not past it.
And so: the result runs straight out of a cause already stated, joined to it as one continuous line.
Auxiliary before a verb: marks the action as the fitting thing to do — what the situation calls for and what falls to the subject (should, ought to).
Auxiliary before a verb: marks the action as the right thing for the subject to do — ought to, should, is supposed to.
Names a cause as a compact noun phrase — owing to X — and lets the result follow on its own.
Cause and effect: the reason, then the result it produces.
Marks a point in time and looks back from it: standing alone it means before now (in the past); placed after an event it means before that event.
Refers back to the text or statements already given: 以上的 + noun is the just-mentioned thing, and 以上 standing alone is the whole of what precedes.
A question word stops asking and means any one at all; the paired 都 (or 也) then sweeps every filler of that slot under one verdict — anyone, anything, anywhere, all alike.
Two states or actions swing back and forth in quick succession: one moment this, the next that.
Measures an action at one short, light instance: do it briefly, give it a quick try, do it once.
Names the instrument or means before the main verb: take up X, then do the action with it.
From: marks the point a change or process sets out from, in a written or formal span.
Names the party an action sets out from: the one who carries it out and answers for it.
Measures A against B's degree of a quality: A reaches as much of X as B has, with 這麼/那麼 pointing at that degree.
Adds a second finished action onto a first by the same subject: did one thing, then did another too.
adv — sets a counter-fact (usually a negation 沒/不) against the other side's claim or worry, dismissing it; placed before that negated verb, often closing on a rhetorical question.
adv — marks an event as a repeat of one that already happened; placed before the verb.
Stacks two qualities or states that hold of one subject at the same time.
Stands on the fact just stated and draws the conclusion that follows from it: 可知 one can know, 看來 it appears, 可見 one can see — the inference grows out of the evidence on the line before.
Partition: of one group, some do this and some do that.
Especially: a set is already in view, and one member of it gets raised above the rest.
Rhetorical dismissal: poses 'is there any worth in V-ing / being SV?' so the framing itself answers none, and the action or state is brushed aside as pointless.
Splits one recurring situation into two cases that take turns: on some occasions it is the first, on others the second.
Marks a small, usually unwelcome, degree of a quality before the adjective: a bit tired, a bit cold.
Heads a cause clause, tracing a situation back to what it sprang from; the result follows, often with 就/也/因此.
Sets B alongside A as the fixed reference, then states how A stands once the two are weighed; formal/written register.
Formal preposition fixing the time or place at which an action happens; slots in front of the verb.
Formal preposition that wedges after the verb (or its auxiliary) to name the place or time an action is sited at; the written-register counterpart of 在.
And (formal): sets two noun-like items side by side as one equal pair, in written or formal speech.
With: in written or formal register, set a second party beside the subject for one shared action.
Contrasts an earlier state of affairs with the different one that has replaced it by now.
Auxiliary before a verb: the subject consents of its own will to do the verb.
Escalation: as the first thing increases, the second increases with it.
One quality climbs on its own as time goes on: more and more, increasingly.
Connective opening the second clause: given the situation just stated, here is the action it led to (and so, thereupon).
Marks one bounded facet of a topic and confines the following remark to that facet: (在) [topic] 方面 = in the area of / on the … side of [topic].
A place-phrase (在 + place-word) sits before 的 and turns into a modifier picking out the noun behind it: the N that is at that place.
Frames a field, aspect, or abstract domain as the ground a statement stands on — internationally, in theory, in this regard.
Concessive degree: push the condition as high as it goes, and the outcome holds anyway.
Casts a circumstance as a weight overhead: 在 opens the condition, 下 sets it above the clause that lives under it.
In the spare room left over beside a main activity: X is the bulk of what one does, and Y happens in the margin alongside it.
Fences a named set as the range, then singles out one member from inside it: (在) [set] (之)中 = among [set], [this one].
Progressive: the verb is underway at this exact moment.
Temporal: 在 + a time word sites the event at that point on the timeline.
Locative: 在 + a place says someone or something is sited there.
Progressive: the action is going on right now.
Warns that continued inaction triggers a looming bad outcome: 再不 projects one more round of NOT doing it forward in time, 就 fires the consequence, 了 marks the new state about to land.
Adds a second supporting factor on top of one already stated; the stack of both produces the result.
conj — adds a second, same-direction reason on top of the first: besides, what's more.
Marks that an action will never happen again: 不 for a future/habitual stop, 沒 for a past stretch in which it has not recurred.
The action was done far earlier than the listener assumes, and it stays settled into the present.
Marks an event as certain to come while leaving its timing open — it will happen sooner or later.
Written clause-internal conjunction: the first clause sets one item, the second clause's topic takes 則 and is gauged by contrast against it (whereas / by contrast / as for).
Question word for manner or method: 怎麼 sits before the verb and asks how an action is carried out.
Fixed question that bundles a whole situation into one matter (一回事) and asks for its identity or explanation: what's going on, what's the story with this.
Fixed question asked when stuck in a predicament, reaching for what to do about it: what now, how do I handle this.
Fixed question probing the cause behind a noticed change or off condition in someone or something: what's the matter, what happened.
Fixed discourse preface: stalls before a hard-to-phrase answer, the speaker asking themselves how to put it while they find the words.
Question word for state or manner. 怎麼樣 sits in the predicate slot where a description would go and asks how something is or how it went.
Resultative: the verb's aim lands and catches. The act succeeded in reaching its result.
照 fixed in front of a verb runs the action by its established way — as agreed, as scheduled, as before — even though the new circumstance would seem to change it.
Carry out an action so it matches a given model — a method, instructions, or a sample; 照 takes the model, 著 binds the doing to it.
Durative: the verb's result rides on its doer and stays worn while life goes on around it.
Two verbs at once: 著 holds the first act open as a running backdrop, and the second verb is what is mainly being done across it.
Durative: an action held in place as an ongoing state.
Discourse connective: re-voice what the other speaker just said as your footing, then draw the next move from it — so, in that case, if you put it that way.
Set 真 before a stative verb to stamp the quality as genuinely so — a felt reaction, not a measured amount.
A-not-A question: offer both answers and let the listener pick.
Opens a clause citing a known or stated reference, then asserts that what follows matches it exactly — 'just as X said / guessed / showed.'
Takes the situation just made true and marks the consequence it now brings about in one decisive stroke — now, with that, at last.
Takes the situation just set up as a given and projects the consequence it now makes possible.
Literary linker: binds a modifier to a noun, the classical 的, kept in fixed and written phrases.
Connects two endpoints of a span; the value runs from the first up to the second, X 至 Y.
Marks the one course left once the alternatives are shut off: have no choice but to, can only.
Adverb marking a stretch that runs from some past start all the way up to the present moment, with the situation still holding now.
Keep the prior claim standing, then attach the one minor thing that qualifies it without overturning it.
Names a result first, then supplies the cause behind it: the reason X holds is Y.
Sufficient condition: this one thing is all it takes, and then the result follows.
Exclusive condition: the result holds only when this one condition is met, and no other way reaches it.
Sets the matter just spoken of to rest and turns the attention onward to a second, related matter named right after it.
Suffixed to a verb to mark the action as ongoing, caught mid-process — the status-label sense of 'in V / under V'.
Resultative on an action verb: the act caught hold of its object and fixed it in place, so it cannot slip away.
A stative verb said twice (AABB for two syllables, AA for one), closed with 地, paints a saturated manner onto the verb that follows.
Topic-comment: lead with the thing under discussion, then comment on it.
Singles out the principal reason, purpose, or factor from several, foregrounding it as the dominant one while smaller ones stay implied.
Bound suffix on a verb of issuing (出/來/源/引/選/譯/摘 …); 自 attaches behind the verb and names the source the thing came out of.
Marks a past starting event and the stretch of time running from it up to now.
Fixes a past starting point and runs the whole stretch from it up to the present: ever since X, the state has held.
Sets off the closing line that binds everything already said into one point.
Gathers everything raised so far and binds it into one summarizing statement that supersedes the detail.
Approximate range: 左右 trails a number-and-measure to bracket it on both sides — the true amount lands a little under or a little over, around X.
